goal: Sign-in not persisting after Google OAuth; add a top-right avatar/signed-in indicator beside the coin chip
done: (1) SIGN-IN: loginWithProvider's redirectTo was window.location.href — the FULL url with volatile query/hash, which commonly fails Supabase's Redirect-URL allow-list so it falls back to the Site URL and the session never lands. Changed redirectTo to a clean, stable, allow-list-friendly URL: localized /profile.html (also where the user sees they're signed in). (2) AVATAR: added a top-right cluster (#mt-top-chips) holding an avatar chip + the coin chip side by side. Avatar (round, coin-chip height) shows the Google avatar or a fallback profile glyph when SIGNED IN, hidden when signed out; reactive via subscribeAuthState; taps to Profile. Moved .mt-coin-chip out of fixed-positioning into the cluster. Verified real browser (signed-out hidden; simulated signed-in glyph shows left of coin). Cache-bust 20260623e -> 20260623f; www synced.
fix: OAuth redirectTo → clean /profile.html; top-right avatar chip beside coin, reactive to auth.
verified: real-browser profile signed-out: cluster top-right, coin chip inside, avatar hidden
real-browser simulated signed-in: avatar glyph shows left of coin
filed: If sign-in STILL fails after this, remaining cause = Supabase dashboard (dzgjjvuiliickdgzshjr): Site URL = https://www.meowtarot.com; Redirect URLs include https://www.meowtarot.com/** + https://meowtarot.com/**. The avatar is the live signal: land on Profile post-Google; no avatar = session didn't establish = dashboard fix.
